description: Classify FX regimes along the IMF spectrum — dollarization, currency board, fixed peg, crawling peg, managed float, free float — and trace their monetary policy implications

fields.name: regime
fields.label: dollarization | monetary_union | currency_board | fixed_peg | crawling_peg | managed_float | free_float

regime_spectrum.dollarization: Foreign currency is legal tender — no domestic monetary policy
regime_spectrum.monetary_union: Shared currency with other members (e.g., euro)
regime_spectrum.currency_board: Domestic currency backed 1:1 by foreign reserves at a fixed rate
regime_spectrum.fixed_peg: Central bank pegs to another currency or basket with narrow band
regime_spectrum.crawling_peg: Peg that adjusts on a pre-announced schedule
regime_spectrum.managed_float: Floats but central bank intervenes to smooth volatility

trilemma.statement: A country cannot simultaneously have fixed FX, free capital mobility, and independent monetary policy
trilemma.implication: Regime choice trades off one of the three

advantages_disadvantages.fixed_adv: Price stability, import-discipline, anchor for inflation expectations
advantages_disadvantages.fixed_disadv: Surrenders monetary independence; vulnerable to speculative attack
advantages_disadvantages.float_adv: Buffer for shocks, retains monetary independence

applications.emerging_markets: Often managed floats with occasional interventions
applications.crisis_dynamics: Fixed regimes can break under capital flight (e.g., GBP 1992, Thai baht 1997)

classify_regime.description: Map regime to monetary policy implications

errors.message: regime must be a valid IMF exchange rate regime

anti_patterns.rule: Do not assume a peg is permanent
anti_patterns.why: Pegs break under persistent imbalances; cost of defending can exceed benefits

anti_patterns.rule: Do not treat managed float interventions as transparent
anti_patterns.why: Central banks rarely disclose intervention thresholds; positioning must tolerate ambiguity
Policy choices that ignore the impossible trinity trigger crises; analyse regime consistency

anti_patterns.rule: Do not apply free-float FX models to pegged economies
anti_patterns.why: Volatility compression under a peg biases risk metrics; regime shifts need stress tests

goals.description: Classify FX regimes along the IMF spectrum — dollarization, currency board, fixed peg, crawling peg, managed float, free float — and trace their monetary policy implications

success_metrics.measurement: All decisions have complete audit trails

constraints.description: All operations must be auditable and traceable

tradeoffs.reason: trading operations require precise execution and full audit trails

invariants: error messages never expose internal system details
